title: Neomammillaria napina
taxonomy: Neomammillaria napina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It is part of the genus Neomammillaria, which includes various species of small cacti. The genus Neomammillaria is a synonym for the genus Mammillaria. Neomammillaria napina is native to Mexico and is known for its small, rounded stems and white flowers. The classification of Neomammillaria napina has been subject to revision, with some sources listing it under the genus Mammillaria.

intro:

Mammillaria napina, commonly known as the dwarf pincushion cactus, is a small, globular cactus species native to Mexico. The health benefits of Mammillaria napina include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of Mammillaria napina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mammillaria napina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as colds, fever, and skin infections.
